items.question: Who is responsible for ensuring train/serve parity after feature selection?
items.answer: ML engineers and MLOps owners who serialize and deploy the full Pipeline (or equivalent artifact) are responsible; data engineers align feature-store schemas to the selector's output mask so inference reads the same columns training used.

items.question: What is the leakage-safe way to combine SelectKBest with cross-validation?
items.answer: Nest SelectKBest inside a sklearn Pipeline with the classifier, then call cross_val_score on the pipeline so each fold refits the selector on training rows only and transforms validation rows with that fold's fitted mask.

items.question: When should you use a filter stage before RFE in production?
items.answer: Use a filter first when you have hundreds or thousands of columns and wrapper cost is prohibitive—drop constant or near-zero variance columns and keep top MI or chi-square features to a manageable pool before RFECV.

items.question: Where does feature selection sit relative to imputation and scaling?
items.answer: Typically after basic cleaning and often after scaling for linear-model-based selectors; the whole chain belongs in one Pipeline so order is fixed: impute → scale → select → train, with fit confined to training data at each step.

items.question: Why is tuning k on the test set dangerous for feature selection pipelines?
items.answer: Test labels then influence which columns enter the model, leaking information into subset choice and making reported test metrics optimistically biased relative to true production generalization.

items.question: How do you deploy the result of embedded SelectFromModel with a Random Forest?
items.answer: Fit a Pipeline containing SelectFromModel and the forest on training data, serialize the fitted pipeline, and at inference call predict on new rows—the selector step applies the same importance threshold and column mask learned during training.

items.question: Cause: SelectKBest is fit on concatenated train and test before splitting. Effect?
items.answer: Test-label statistics influence which features are kept, causing data leakage and validation or test scores that overstate real-world performance until the selector is refit inside train-only folds.

items.question: Cause: Production serving uses a manual column list from an old notebook. Effect?
items.answer: Retrained models may expect a different support mask, producing silent feature misalignment, wrong predictions, or runtime errors when schema and artifact diverge.

items.question: What if your feature store adds 20 new columns but the deployed selector mask is unchanged?
items.answer: The new columns are ignored at inference unless you retrain; if serving code passes all columns without the fitted selector, you get train/serve skew—retrain the pipeline or enforce the stored support mask in the ingestion layer.

items.question: What if RFECV picks 8 features but business requires at least 15 interpretable inputs?
items.answer: Constrain the search with min_features_to_select, switch to a filter keeping top 15 by MI, or combine domain-mandatory columns with data-driven selection—then validate held-out metrics under the business constraint.
